quick_skim.title: FlashCP: Load-Balanced Communication-Efficient Context Parallelism for LLM Training
quick_skim.abstract: Context parallelism (CP) is essential for training large-scale, long-context language models, as it partitions sequences to reduce memory overhead. However, existing CP methods suffer from workload imbalance, inefficient kernels, and redundant communication due to static sequence sharding and key-value (KV) tensor communication. We present FlashCP, a load-balanced and communication-efficient framework for CP training. FlashCP introduces a sharding-aware communication mechanism to eliminate redundant KV communication and proposes a novel Whole-Doc sharding strategy that maximizes communication savings while maintaining balanced workloads. To efficiently combine Whole-Doc and Per-Doc sharding, FlashCP further designs a heuristic algorithm to search for near-optimal sharding plans. Extensive experiments show that FlashCP achieves up to 1.63x speedup over state-of-the-art CP frameworks across diverse datasets.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: load-balanced communication-efficient context parallelism for LLM training
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于LLM训练的负载均衡通信高效上下文并行
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于LLM训练的负载均衡通信高效上下文并行
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于LLM训练的负载均衡通信高效上下文并行
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: FlashCP introduces load-balanced and communication-efficient context parallelism for long-context LLM training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: FlashCP为长上下文LLM训练引入了负载均衡且通信高效的上下文并行机制。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: FlashCP为长上下文LLM训练引入了负载均衡且通信高效的上下文并行机制。

quick_skim.title: Resource-aware Computation-Communication Overlap for multi-GPU ML Workloads
quick_skim.abstract: The rapid growth of large-scale machine learning (ML) has made distributed training across multiple GPUs a fundamental component of modern ML systems. As model sizes and computational throughput continue to increase, communication overhead has become a dominant bottleneck in multi-GPU training, particularly when computation and communication are executed sequentially. This work explores concurrent execution of computation and collective communication using two portable runtime controls: shared-memory-driven occupancy shaping for computation kernels and elevated scheduling priority for communication kernels. Our approach regulates computation-kernel residency through per-block shared-memory allocation, leaving sufficient on-chip resources for communication kernels to make progress. In addition, assigning higher priority to communication streams ensures steady communication progress once resources become available. Experiments on NVIDIA A40, A100, H100, and AMD MI250X GPUs demonstrate that the proposed method enables effective computation-communication overlap and reduces total execution time by up to 25.5 percent, without modifying vendor libraries or kernel implementations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: computation-communication overlap for multi-GPU distributed training
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多GPU分布式训练中的计算通信重叠
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多GPU分布式训练中的计算通信重叠
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多GPU分布式训练中的计算通信重叠
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes concurrent execution of computation and communication to reduce overhead in multi-GPU ML training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 提出在多GPU机器学习训练中并发执行计算与通信以减少开销。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 提出在多GPU机器学习训练中并发执行计算与通信以减少开销。
